# S.T.A.N.D Mentorship — News, Media Coverage & Awards Research

**Subject:** S.T.A.N.D Mentorship ("Stand Mentorship"), a youth-mentorship nonprofit in Maryland (Baltimore City + Howard County), and founder/executive director **Tigana S. Duncan** ("Coach Duncan").
**Website:** standmentorship.org · **Official socials:** Facebook `standmentorship1`, Instagram (linked from site)
**Research date:** 2026-06-17 · **Researcher:** media-research sweep (web search + page fetches)

---

## Executive summary (the headline finding)

**Independent press coverage of this organization is very thin.** After an exhaustive sweep of every priority outlet (Baltimore Sun, The Baltimore Banner, AFRO, Baltimore Times, WBAL-TV, WJZ/CBS Baltimore, WMAR-2, FOX45/WBFF, WYPR, Maryland Daily Record, Howard County Times, Columbia Flier, Baltimore Magazine, Baltimore's Child), the org's verifiable public footprint comes from just **three buckets**:

1. **WMAR-2 News (ABC Baltimore)** — the *only* traditional news outlet with confirmed coverage; two TV/web stories on the org's Baltimore father-son basketball game (~2021 and ~2022).
2. **Howard County Government press releases** — the org is named repeatedly (grant recipient, $100K budget line, founder appointed to a county council, summer-program results). This is *official recognition*, not journalism.
3. **Columbia Community Care (a partner nonprofit) blog** — two flattering profile/feature pieces. Earned-via-partnership, not independent press.

The single most notable *award* is being the **inaugural grant recipient of the Black Philanthropy Circle of Howard County** (Jan 2026).

There is **no** confirmed coverage in the major Baltimore dailies/weeklies (Sun, Banner, AFRO, Baltimore Times), no other TV station, no radio, no podcast, no "40 Under 40," and **no city/county proclamation** found. The org's own website has **no press/news/awards page** — a clear gap for the redesign.

> ⚠️ **Disambiguation handled throughout.** Many unrelated "STAND" entities exist (Operation Homefront's "United We Stand," United We Stand PR, etc.), and several unrelated "Duncans" (Doug Duncan, John D.C. Duncan, Tim Duncan's son). Items below are tied to **this** org only. One WMAR look-alike ("Fathers Fighting 4 Fathers") was explicitly **excluded** after verification. AFRO mentorship round-ups and a Calvin Ball AFRO op-ed were checked and do **NOT** name STAND.

> 🔤 **Two acronym expansions are both in use for the same org/founder:** "**Stepping Towards a New Destiny**" (also styled "Stepping Toward a New Destiny Corporation"; this is the legal/LinkedIn name) **and** "**Strengthening, Training, Advancing, Nurturing, Developing**" (the mission-statement backronym on the website). Same Tigana Duncan, same team — not two organizations.

---

## (a) Chronological media-coverage list

### 1. WMAR-2 News (ABC Baltimore) — "Father Son Basketball game brings community outside the lines"
- **Date:** ~June 2021 (search index reports June 19, 2021; Father's Day-weekend timing fits) — *date confidence: MEDIUM*
- **URL:** https://www.wmar2news.com/news/region/baltimore-city/father-son-basketball-game-brings-community-outside-the-lines
- **Summary:** TV/web news story on S.T.A.N.D Mentorship's father-son basketball game at the Robert C. Marshall Rec Center in Upton, Baltimore. Frames the event around providing male role models to boys without present fathers. Features community figures Dr. Andrey Bundley, Andrew Muhammad, commentator "Big Fred" Watkins, and griller Mike Battle. Quote: *"If we can do this in one place in Baltimore, we can do this in a thousand places."* (Bundley).
- **Org-match confidence: HIGH** (explicitly "hosted by S.T.A.N.D Mentorship," founder Tigana Duncan).

### 2. WMAR-2 News (ABC Baltimore) — "Annual fathers-son basketball game empowers Baltimore youth, celebrates dads"
- **Date:** ~June 2022 (search index reports June 17, 2022) — *date confidence: MEDIUM* (one automated fetch mis-guessed "2026"; treat the year cautiously)
- **URL:** https://www.wmar2news.com/news/local-news/annual-fathers-son-basketball-game-empowers-baltimore-youth-celebrates-dads
- **Summary:** Follow-up story on the recurring father-son basketball tournament. Tigana Duncan explains he founded S.T.A.N.D because his own father was largely absent; the event is "about changing their perspective, but first, challenging their perspective." Notes the "sons" team's winning streak and Duncan's bond with his son Jeremiah.
- **Org-match confidence: HIGH.**

### 3. Columbia Community Care (partner-org blog) — "Harnessing the Potential Within"
- **Date:** March 26, 2024
- **URL:** https://columbiacommunitycare.org/harnessing-the-potential-within/
- **Summary:** Feature profile of Tigana Duncan and S.T.A.N.D (Stepping Towards a New Destiny), a male youth-mentoring program in Howard County. Centers his compassion-driven philosophy ("People need hope. They need a lifeline."), a story of a mentee who reconnected at 21 to say thanks, and a call for more mentors and community funding.
- **Confidence: HIGH** that it's about this org. *Caveat:* this is a **partner nonprofit's own blog**, not independent journalism — tone is promotional.

### 4. Howard County Government — "Calvin Ball Announces Bold Investments to Launch and Expand Transformational Youth Programming" (News040924)
- **Date:** April 9, 2024
- **URL:** https://www.howardcountymd.gov/News040924
- **Summary:** Announces $820,000 in FY25 youth-programming investments. STAND is named **in passing** as one of the Columbia Community Care programs that will benefit from "The Source" community center (the former Columbia Flier building). Tigana Duncan not named.
- **Confidence: MEDIUM** (brief mention; STAND named, founder not).

### 5. Howard County Government — Round-2 YEP! grant recipients (News021325)
- **Date:** February 13, 2025
- **URL:** https://www.howardcountymd.gov/News021325
- **Summary:** "STAND Mentorship Program" listed among 33 organizations awarded second-round Youth Engagement Programming (YEP!) grants under County Executive Calvin Ball. Individual award amounts not broken out. (Note: STAND was **not** in the April 28, 2023 first-round list — verified — so funding began with this cycle.)
- **Confidence: HIGH.**

### 6. Howard County Government — "Calvin Ball Establishes Youth Engagement Strategy (YES!) Council" (News033125)
- **Date:** March 31, 2025
- **URL:** https://www.howardcountymd.gov/News033125
- **Summary:** Announces the YES! Council to address youth violence. States **$100,000 to STAND and PUSH in the FY-year budget**, on top of ~$250,000 invested over two years; the programs use a community-based violence-prevention approach and served ~100 young people. **Tigana Duncan is quoted as representing STAND Mentorship**: *"One of the things I find very important is equity for all kids…we can't forget that one percent of our kids that may not even have hope for tomorrow."* (Implies a council seat/role.)
- **Confidence: HIGH.** Strongest *official* recognition found (org named, founder quoted as a community voice, dedicated funding).

### 7. Columbia Community Care (partner-org blog) — "Summer Youth Program Prevents Crime and Strengthens Self-Worth"
- **Date:** ~August–September 2025 (covers the program that ended Aug 9, 2025)
- **URL:** https://columbiacommunitycare.org/summer-youth-program-prevents-crime-and-strengthens-self-worth/
- **Summary:** In-depth feature on the **"United We STAND"** six-week summer program — a collaboration between STAND's Tigana "Coach" Duncan and Saquan Maxwell's **PUSH** program — serving 32 teens (high achievers, court-referred, and at-risk). Offered life/communication skills, financial literacy, mentorship, career insights, daily meals, and **$100 stipends**, plus trips (Hershey Park, Ocean City, paintball, go-karts). **Reported outcomes: zero new arrests/repeat offenses among court-referred teens; improved financial-literacy and "social IQ" on pre/post assessment.** Confirms STAND was founded "over 10 years ago in Baltimore City" and **expanded to Howard County in 2015**; partners with the county court system, state's attorney, and sheriff to reduce recidivism.
- **Confidence: HIGH** (org/founder). *Caveat:* partner-org blog, promotional tone.

### 8. Howard County Government — "Calvin Ball Highlights Success of Summer Youth Programs; Announces FY26 YEP! Grants" (News090325a)
- **Date:** September 3, 2025
- **URL:** https://www.howardcountymd.gov/News090325a
- **Summary:** Highlights the **STAND Summer Program** as a six-week initiative for at-risk youth ages 12–18 (referred from the Homewood Center, Dept. of Juvenile Services, Passages Program, Wilde Lake HS). Reports a **96% average attendance rate**, with **92% of participants reporting feeling "more confident" and "more supported."** Tigana Duncan not named in this release.
- **Confidence: HIGH** (org named with specific program data).

---

### Event listings / community posts (not press, but public-facing coverage)

- **Wilde Lake Community Association — event listing:** "S.T.A.N.D. Mentorship Presents: Father/Son Basketball Game at WLMS, June 29." **Date of event: June 29, 2024, 12:00–2:30 pm**, Wilde Lake Middle School, Columbia. URL: https://www.wildelake.org/single-cal-event/s-t-a-n-d-mentorship-presents-father-son-basketball-game-at-wlms-june-29/ — *Confidence: HIGH.* (Shows the father-son event recurs and has moved to Howard County.)
- **MD State Delegate Jessica Feldmark (District 12) — Facebook post:** "So pleased to visit STAND Mentorship today at their father-son basketball game…" URL: https://m.facebook.com/JessicaFeldmarkforD12/posts/.../867142601901720/ — *Confidence: MEDIUM* (full text could not be retrieved via fetch; based on search snippet). A sitting state legislator publicly visiting/endorsing the org = useful reputational signal.

---

## (b) Awards & recognitions

### ⭐ Black Philanthropy Circle of Howard County — First-Ever Grant (most significant award found)
- **Date:** January 2, 2026
- **Awarded by:** Black Philanthropy Circle of Howard County (BPCHoCo), a fund of the **Community Foundation of Howard County** (founded Dec 2023).
- **Recipient:** "S.T.A.N.D. (Stepping Towards a New Destiny) Mentorship Program," Tigana Duncan, Founder & Executive Director.
- **Amount:** not disclosed in the announcement.
- **URL:** https://cfhoco.org/black-philanthropy-circle-of-howard-county-awards-first-ever-grant-to-s-t-a-n-d-mentorship-program/
- **Why it matters:** STAND was chosen as the **very first** grantee of this new community-philanthropy circle — a distinct honor/endorsement. Chair/founder Derrick Leak and CFHC CEO Melissa Curtin are quoted.
- **Confidence: HIGH.**

### Howard County YEP! Grant + dedicated budget allocation (government recognition)
- Round-2 YEP! grant recipient (Feb 13, 2025) **and** part of a **$100,000 FY-budget allocation to STAND & PUSH** (Mar 31, 2025), on top of ~$250K over two prior years. — *Confidence: HIGH.* See media items #5 and #6.

### Civic appointment — Howard County Youth Engagement Strategy (YES!) Council
- Tigana Duncan participated/was quoted as a STAND representative at the YES! Council launch (Mar 31, 2025), indicating an advisory role on a county council addressing youth violence. — *Confidence: MEDIUM–HIGH* (role implied by quote and program funding; exact appointment title not confirmed).

### NOT found (searched, none located)
- No "40 Under 40," no Daily Record "Leading Women/Most Admired CEOs" listing for Duncan, no Baltimore City Council citation, no mayoral/County-Executive **proclamation**, no "community hero" award, no Candid/GuideStar "Seal of Transparency" or GreatNonprofits reviews attributable to this org. *(Confidence that these don't exist: MEDIUM — absence of search hits, not proof of absence.)*

---

## (c) Interviews / podcasts / video

- **YouTube — "Project 365 Day 204 — Tigana Duncan / Coach Duncan"** and **"Project 365 Day 205 — Coach Tigana Duncan"** (≈ July 23–24, 2021), themed *"Investing in Our Youth…Priceless!"* Part 1 & 2.
  - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wxSDrJ1VAXs
  - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=YqnaXE0L2SQ
  - *Confidence: MEDIUM–HIGH* that this is the same Coach Duncan. "Project 365" appears to be a personal daily-vlog series rather than a media outlet — treat as a video appearance, not press.
- **WMAR-2 News TV segments** — the two father-son basketball stories (items #1–2) are TV news packages (video + web).
- **Facebook video (official):** STAND's own page (`standmentorship1`) posted a clip thanking Saquan Maxwell for accepting an invitation as a speaker — confirms active social-media presence/programming, not external media.
- **No dedicated podcast episode or radio interview found** featuring Duncan or the org. *(Confidence: MEDIUM.)*

---

## (d) Reputation / tone

- **Tone is uniformly positive** wherever the org appears: youth violence prevention, fatherhood/male role models, second chances, recidivism reduction, financial literacy, and trades/career exposure for "young men of color." A representative founder quote on the trades angle: *"By the time kids are in middle school, if you expose them to trades and innovation, and you expose them to college tours, now you are setting before them a path."*
- **The credible validators are institutional, not media:** Howard County Government (repeated naming + funding + a council role), the Community Foundation of Howard County / Black Philanthropy Circle, and a sitting state delegate. That's meaningful credibility — but it is **earned recognition and funding**, not press attention.
- **Independent journalism is the gap.** Only WMAR-2 has covered them, and only for the Baltimore father-son event years ago. The richest narrative pieces ("Harnessing the Potential Within," the United We STAND feature) are published by **partner org Columbia Community Care's own blog**, so they read as promotional and shouldn't be presented as third-party press.
- **Operating relationship to note:** STAND is closely intertwined with **Columbia Community Care (CCC)** in Howard County — programs run at/through CCC's Youth & Teen Center and "The Source," and the "United We STAND" summer program is a STAND + PUSH collaboration. The Baltimore City roots (≈2014, father-son games, Orioles/early events) and the Howard County expansion (2015→present) are two phases of the same org.
- **Website implication for the redesign:** standmentorship.org currently has **no Press / News / In-the-Media / Awards page and no partner logos**. Given that real, citable recognition exists (Black Philanthropy Circle grant, Howard County funding/quotes, WMAR coverage, a delegate's endorsement, measurable outcomes like 96% attendance / zero re-arrests), building a "Recognition & Impact" section is a quick, high-credibility win.

---

## Items investigated and EXCLUDED (mis-identification guard)

- **WMAR "Local fatherhood mentorship program highlights fathers and father figures"** → about **"Fathers Fighting 4 Fathers"**, NOT STAND. *Excluded.*
- **AFRO "How Baltimore mentorship programs are closing the racial income gap for Black boys"** and **Calvin Ball's AFRO op-ed "Investing in our young people" (Sep 6, 2025)** → verified, **do not name STAND/Duncan**. *Excluded.*
- **Howard County first-round YEP! grants (Apr 28, 2023, News042823)** → STAND **not** on that list. *Excluded as a recognition for that year.*
- Unrelated "STAND"/"United We Stand" orgs (Operation Homefront, United We Stand PR) and unrelated "Duncans" (Doug Duncan, John D.C. Duncan, Tim Duncan's son, athletic coaches in Trinidad & Tobago) → *Excluded.*
- The novel *Tigana* by Guy Gavriel Kay repeatedly polluted name searches → *noise, excluded.*

---
